event: Memorial to the Unknown British Soldier
symbols: flag-draped coffin — the anonymous sacrifice of the common soldier, every life known to God
grand empty hall — the vastness of loss and the hollow grandeur of worldly power before death
open dark doorway — the mystery of death, the threshold into the unknown
chandelier and gilded walls — the opulence of state contrasted with the humble anonymity of the fallen
description: A solitary coffin draped in the Union Jack lies on a checkered marble floor in the center of an ornate hall, likely the Hall of Mirrors at Versailles. The grand interior features gilded walls with decorative panels, busts on pedestals, and a large crystal chandelier overhead. A dark arched doorway opens at the center rear, creating a dramatic vanishing point. The vast, empty space around the small coffin creates a powerful contrast between the grandeur of the setting and the anonymous humility of the soldier's remains.

_meta.title: To the Unknown British Soldier in France
